mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving equity valuations higher through elevated earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.50% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.3%, real rates turn negative, incentivizing equity allocation as a real asset hedge and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.26% and CPIAUCSL at 208.028 as of 2013-01-15
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with inflation emergence
failure_condition: Trigger condition occurs but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1,500 thousand units signals accelerating residential investment and construction employment, which multiplies through the economy to boost aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Trigger fires but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling demand destruction and recessionary pressure that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.8604 and UNRATE at 4.6% as of 2013-01-15
regime_hint: demand shock or early recession signal
failure_condition: Trigger condition occurs but 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a 3-month moving average decline of more than 1.5% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, indicating consumer demand weakness that signals earnings headwinds and produces negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month MA decline > 1.5% AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 376051.0 and GDPC1 at 11507.9 as of 2013-01-15
failure_condition: Trigger fires but 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market tightening that raises wage growth expectations and corporate cost pressures, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: Trigger condition occurs but 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates to year-over-year growth exceeding 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains unchanged for 6+ months, signaling monetary policy lag and expected future tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for 6+ months
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation emergence
failure_condition: Trigger fires but 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumption that cascades into negative earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1467.0 and RSAFS at 376051.0 as of 2013-01-15
failure_condition: Trigger condition occurs but 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
